items.title: Spot Prices Wrap: Natural Gas Cash Weakens as Demand Hits 10-Day Low
items.summary: Physical natural gas prices for Saturday through Monday delivery fell at most locations on Friday amid faltering demand as temperatures warmed.

items.title: Futures Settle: Natural Gas Slips as Weak Fundamentals Outweigh Middle East Chaos
items.summary: U.S. natural gas futures softened on Friday, steered lower by bearish demand and supply signals despite mounting anxiety over the destruction of vital gas infrastructure in the Middle East.

items.title: MidDay Snapshot: Cash Natural Gas Weakens on Broad Demand Erosion
items.summary: Cash natural gas prices trended mostly lower in the weekís final session as SaturdayĖMonday trading reflected softer demand. Milder weather and weekend business closures pressured residential, commercial and industrial consumption.

items.title: North Field Expansion Project Delays Likely as Qatari LNG Output Crippled by Iranian Attacks
items.summary: QatarEnergy expects the Iranian missile attacks on its Ras Laffan Industrial City earlier this week to cost it roughly $20 billion in annual revenue until the damage is repaired.

items.title: Middle East War Jolted Global Natural Gas Prices, Could Henry Hub Spike Too?
items.summary: The war in the Middle East has caused global shockwaves in the energy world, but the impact on U.S. natural gas prices has been somewhat muted so far due to record domestic production and a healthy supply/demand balance.

items.title: Natural Gas Forward Prices Slide as Strong Supply, Spring Eclipse Mideast Risk
items.summary: Strong production, healthy storage inventories and the fast approaching shoulder season have outweighed the uncertainties of an escalating conflict in the Middle East and dragged down U.S. natural gas forward prices.

items.title: Midmorning Markets: Outsized South Central Natural Gas Storage Injection Pressures Permian Prices
items.summary: Operators in the South Central region got an early ó and significant ó start to the natural gas storage injection season, the latest government inventory data showed. A deficit of supply compared with historical norms narrowed by 30% and laid another

items.title: What Does a 60-Day Suspension of the Jones Act Mean for U.S. LNG?
items.summary: As energy markets shift to adapt to extended supply disruptions in the Middle East, industry and economic experts are tempering expectations for a policy fix for rising fuel prices.

items.title: US Drillers Add Oil Rigs For Second Week In A Row As Prices Soar
items.summary: The total number of active drilling rigs for oil and gas in the United States fell this week, according to new data that Baker Hughes published on Friday, bringing the total rig count in the US to 552 this week, down 41 from this same time last year.

items.title: Oil Prices Ease As US Pulls Out All Stops To Secure Supply

items.title: U.S. Bets on Natural Gas to Power 10 GW AI Buildout in Ohio

items.title: Why the Global Oil System Cannot Replace Hormuz Flows

items.title: Asian Refiners Pay Record Premiums for Non-Middle East Crude
items.summary: Refiners in Asia are paying huge premiums for crude that could replace some of the supply stuck in the Middle East, with the most suitable grades from Norway and the U.S. being bid at record-high double-digit premiums over Dated Brent.† Many refineri

items.title: Oil Extends Rally as Hormuz Stays Closed
items.summary: Oil is heading for a fifth straight weekly gain, with Brent near $109, as the Strait of Hormuz remains shut for a third week, keeping supply tight despite policy efforts to ease the market. Friday, March 20, 2026 Oil markets are set to post their fif

items.title: European Gas Price Set for 20% Weekly Jump on Qatarís LNG Outage
